questions.q: Whose signet ring is carried as a token of truth in this episode?
questions.explanation: The ring belonged to Rama and bore his name, serving as proof of his messenger.

questions.q: Who carried Rama's ring across the ocean to Lanka?
questions.explanation: Hanuman leaped to Lanka bearing Rama's ring to identify himself to Sita.

questions.q: To whom did Hanuman deliver Rama's ring?
questions.explanation: Hanuman gave the ring to Sita as a sign that he truly came from Rama.

questions.q: In which grove was Sita held captive when she received the ring?
questions.explanation: Sita was kept in the Ashoka Vatika (Ashoka grove) in Lanka, where Hanuman found her.

questions.q: What was the main purpose of Rama sending the ring with Hanuman?
options: To prove the messenger truly came from Rama
To pay a ransom for Sita
To mark a boundary in Lanka
questions.explanation: The ring served as a credible token so Sita would trust that Hanuman was Rama's envoy.

questions.q: Who was holding Sita captive in Lanka?
questions.explanation: Ravana, the rakshasa king of Lanka, had abducted Sita and held her captive.

questions.q: What token did Sita give Hanuman to carry back to Rama in return?
questions.explanation: Sita answered the ring by sending her own hair-jewel, the Chudamani, back to Rama.

questions.q: What kind of object is Rama's signet ring described as in this card?
options: An artifact and token of truth
questions.explanation: The card classifies the ring as an artifact, specifically a token of truth between Rama and Sita.

questions.q: Hanuman is a devoted servant of which prince?
questions.explanation: Hanuman is renowned as the supremely devoted servant and messenger of Rama.

questions.q: Crossing what obstacle did Hanuman reach Lanka to deliver the ring?
questions.explanation: Hanuman leaped across the ocean to reach the island of Lanka.

questions.q: What name was engraved or inscribed on the ring that helped Sita recognize it?
questions.explanation: The ring bore Rama's name, which assured Sita of its authenticity.

questions.q: In which kanda (book) of the Valmiki Ramayana does Hanuman deliver the ring to Sita?
questions.explanation: Hanuman's journey to Lanka and meeting with Sita is told in the Sundara Kanda.

questions.q: From which tree did Hanuman first observe Sita before revealing the ring?
questions.explanation: Hanuman hid in a simshapa tree in the Ashoka grove to watch Sita before approaching her.

questions.q: Why did Hanuman show the ring before speaking at length with Sita?
options: To reassure Sita he was no trick of Ravana
questions.explanation: Sita feared Ravana might be deceiving her in disguise, so the ring proved Hanuman was Rama's genuine envoy.

questions.q: Who was the king of the vanaras whose army Hanuman served when sent to find Sita?
questions.explanation: Sugriva, allied with Rama, dispatched the vanara search parties, among whom Hanuman went to Lanka.

questions.q: What emotional reaction did Sita have on receiving Rama's ring?
questions.explanation: Seeing Rama's ring filled the long-grieving Sita with joy and renewed hope.

questions.q: Which rakshasi treated Sita kindly and interpreted favorable omens during her captivity?
questions.explanation: Trijata, a rakshasi, was gentle toward Sita and foretold Rama's victory through her dream.

questions.q: Besides delivering the ring, what did Hanuman offer to do for Sita that she declined?
options: Carry her back to Rama on his shoulders
questions.explanation: Hanuman offered to carry Sita to Rama, but she declined, preferring Rama to win her honorably.

questions.q: What was Sita's father, who is connected to her by her crest-jewel and lineage?
questions.explanation: Sita was the daughter of King Janaka of Mithila, a detail she used to authenticate her own token.

questions.q: What recognition token did Sita ask Hanuman to recount to Rama, in addition to the Chudamani?
options: A private memory shared only by Rama and Sita
questions.explanation: Sita recounted an intimate event (the crow incident) known only to her and Rama as further proof.

questions.q: After meeting Sita and delivering the ring, what did Hanuman do before leaving Lanka?
options: Wreaked havoc and let his tail be set afire
questions.explanation: Hanuman destroyed the grove and palace, was captured, and set Lanka ablaze with his burning tail.

questions.q: Why was the ring a more immediate proof to Sita than words alone?
options: It was a personal object she recognized as Rama's own
questions.explanation: As Rama's personal signet, the ring was a tangible, recognizable possession that words could not counterfeit.

questions.q: In Valmiki's account, what specific term is often used for the ring inscribed with Rama's name (anguliyaka)?
options: A finger-ring / signet bearing Rama's name
questions.explanation: Valmiki describes it as Rama's anguliyaka, a finger-ring engraved with his name.

questions.q: The crow that wounded Sita, recalled as a secret proof, was associated with which deity in disguise according to tradition?
questions.explanation: Tradition holds the crow was Jayanta, son of Indra, whom Rama punished with a blade of grass.

questions.q: On which mountain peak did Hanuman alight first after crossing the sea toward Lanka?
questions.explanation: Lanka was situated on the heights of Mount Trikuta, where Hanuman arrived after the ocean crossing.

questions.q: What deadline had Ravana given Sita to accept him, a pressure relieved by the ring's arrival?
options: Twelve months (with a recently shortened term)
questions.explanation: Ravana had set Sita a term of months to relent, and the ring brought hope amid that threat.

questions.q: Which sea-creature sent by the gods tried to test or aid Hanuman during his leap, before he reached Lanka?
questions.explanation: Surasa, mother of the nagas, tested Hanuman mid-flight; he passed through her mouth and shrank free.

questions.q: What did Sita fear the ring and messenger might actually be, given Ravana's powers?
options: A forgery created by Ravana's illusion (maya)
questions.explanation: Knowing Ravana's mastery of illusion, Sita initially feared the ring could be another deceptive maya.

questions.q: Which submerged mountain rose to offer Hanuman rest during his ocean leap, out of gratitude to his father's kind?
questions.explanation: Mount Mainaka rose from the sea to let Hanuman rest, but he touched it and pressed on without stopping.

questions.q: What larger purpose did the exchange of ring and crest-jewel ultimately serve in the narrative?
options: To establish a trade treaty with Lanka
To confirm Sita's location and resolve, enabling Rama's rescue campaign
questions.explanation: The tokens confirmed Sita lived and where she was held, galvanizing Rama's war to rescue her.
